Method‐provenance block:
Property: Most electrophilic Fukui function value (f⁺) on nitrobenzene (atom 4, C para to NO₂) = 0.49641782 (dimensionless)
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2‐TZVP (no density fitting, exact four-center integrals) in PySCF v<engine-reported>
Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates (file: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/nitrobenzene/nitrobenzene.xyz); single-point calculations on this fixed geometry for the N, N–1, and N+1 charge states (no re-optimization; protocol noted as B3LYP/def2-TZVP//input-xyz)
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit or explicit solvation)
Charge and multiplicity: neutral singlet parent (Q=0, M=1); cation (Q=+1, M=2); anion (Q=–1, M=2)
Software: chemkit → PySCF backend
Convergence & caveats: All three SCF calculations reported status "ok" (converged). Warnings returned by the engine:
• sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• common header present
Integrity verdict: trustworthy = true

Live log + reproducibility: The engine streamed its output to a live .out file (path printed at run time; e.g. fukui_YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.out). Full JSON results stored at /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmp389gcywg/live.json.

Result: The largest electrophilic Fukui index (f⁺) is 0.49641782 on carbon 4 (C para to the nitro group). This identifies that site as the most susceptible to nucleophilic attack under the chosen DFT level.

Most nucleophilic site: carbon 5 (C meta to NO₂) with f⁻ = 0.1072882 (reported for completeness).

All quantities are dimensionless and specific to the B3LYP/def2-TZVP gas-phase single-point protocol; different functionals, basis sets, solvation models, or geometries will shift the values.
